tasks.title: Update Claude/GLM provider delegation hints with inline TDD. Red: add targeted asset assertion proving Claude no longer contains anti-delegation wording and both Claude/GLM contain hyphenated `adv-engineer` delegation-routing guidance. Green: update `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/claude.md` and `glm.md` so Claude replaces conflicting line, GLM adds guidance, both stay <=20 lines, and no `adv_engineer` spelling appears.
tasks.implementation_summary: Added provider-hint asset test for Claude/GLM delegation guidance. Replaced Claude anti-delegation hint with delegation-routing-aware `adv-engineer` preference. Added matching GLM `adv-engineer` delegation guidance. Verified targeted asset test passes.

error_recovery.last_error: Initial green run failed because test expected `delegation routing` before `adv-engineer`, while first hint wording placed `adv-engineer` first.
error_recovery.next_strategy: Resolved by reordering hint wording to make delegation-routing condition explicit before `adv-engineer`.

attempts.error: GREEN phase exitCode=1: expected provider hints to match /delegation routing.*adv-engineer/s, but hint text said `adv-engineer` before `delegation routing`.
attempts.diagnosis: Implementation intent was correct, but assertion encoded a stricter wording order to emphasize routing condition before sub-agent choice.
attempts.fix_tried: Reworded Claude and GLM hints to say: when delegation routing marks work delegate_allowed/delegate_preferred, prefer spawning adv-engineer.

tasks.title: Run provider assembly verification. Execute targeted provider-hint asset tests and `scripts/sync-global.sh --check` where environment permits. Record exact pass/fail output or blocker; fix only in-scope provider hint/test issues discovered by verification.

tdd_reclassification.reason: Prep approval explicitly called out verification task as separate-verification; task runs post-implementation provider assembly checks rather than owns product code changes.
tdd_reclassification.approval_evidence: User approved prep after handoff stated: `mark verification task as separate-verification`.

tasks.implementation_summary: Ran targeted provider-hint asset test successfully (52 tests). Ran `scripts/sync-global.sh --check`; first combined command hit provider runtime canary timeout, rerun passed all ADV config/tool drift/runtime canary checks.

error_recovery.last_error: First combined verification command exited 1 because `sync-global.sh --check` runtime canary for adv-claude timed out after 30s.
error_recovery.next_strategy: Reran sync check separately; runtime canary resolved all 4 provider ADV prompts and config validation passed.

attempts.error: `scripts/sync-global.sh --check` reported: runtime canary: adv-claude timed out after 30s.
attempts.diagnosis: Transient runtime canary/OpenCode debug timeout; targeted provider test already passed and no static config/tool drift errors were present.

green.test_file: pnpm --dir plugin exec vitest run src/adv-command-routing-assets.test.ts

tasks.completed_by: Verification complete. Checkpoint returned clean at e55fdd96f23872e7c0f16dfb7ca4db311e151a2a.

wisdom.content: In fresh ADV worktrees, `plugin/node_modules` may be absent even when main checkout has deps; install with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` before worktree-local Vitest. For targeted Vitest in this repo, `pnpm exec vitest run <file>` is more reliable than `pnpm test -- <file>` because the latter expanded into broader test execution during this run.

`scripts/sync-global.sh --check` runtime canary can transiently time out on a single provider (`adv-claude timed out after 30s`) while static tool drift checks pass. Rerun the check separately before classifying as failure; in this run the next `--check` resolved all 4 provider prompts and passed.

proposal.notes: User approved proposal after current-state evidence that Claude provider hint discourages sub-agent use and GLM lacks delegation encouragement.

discovery.notes: User approved agreement. Discovery established source files, sync path, canonical adv-engineer spelling, constraints, and acceptance criteria.

design.notes: Design completed after mandatory independent validator. Validator verdict CAUTION, not CONFLICT; required constraints incorporated: replace Claude anti-delegation sentence, use `adv-engineer`, preserve runtime safety, add content-aware tests.

planning.notes: User approved prep/task graph. Two tasks planned: implementation with inline TDD and separate provider assembly verification.

execution.notes: Re-emitting execution completion after workflow repair because adv_change_archive still reported Temporal incomplete gates despite gate_status canArchive true.

acceptance.notes: Re-emitting acceptance completion after workflow repair because adv_change_archive still reported Temporal incomplete gates despite gate_status canArchive true.

release.notes: Re-emitting release completion after workflow repair; user already signed off with `sign off`.
